title: bis quarterly review / equity rotation / market dispersion | Economic Commentary | Markets recalibrate amid shifting currents
summary: The BIS Quarterly Review for March 2026 documents significant shifts in global financial markets during the review period, with investors rotating away from previously high-performing US large cap and growth stocks toward European, Japanese, and emerging market equities. While index-level volatility rose modestly, individual stock volatility increased substantially more. The Middle East conflict in early March triggered reversals in several trends, pushing oil and gas prices higher, causing the US dollar to reverse its depreciation, and pressuring equity gains across European and Asian markets. Credit markets remained broadly stable, though leveraged loans and private credit faced headwinds.
